items.title: Millions of WordPress sites just got hacked... again
items.summary:
Warp is the agentic development environment born out of the terminal. Download Warp for free today at → https://go.warp.dev/fireship

Someone spent $100k buying a massive collection of WordPress plugins and planted a backdoor in all of them. Naturally, CloudFlare stepped in with EmDash: a slop-forked WP alternative that promises to fix plugin security for good.

#coding #programming #wordpress

🔖 Topics Covered
- Wordpress plugin hack 
- EmDash

📚 Resources
- https://www.wordfence.com/threat-int

items.title: Google just casually disrupted the open-source AI narrative…
items.summary:
CodeRabbit CLI can fix your agent’s code before it ever opens a PR - https://coderabbit.link/fireship Free forever for any open source project.

Last week, Google surprised us all by shipping their latest micro model Gemma 4 under a truly open source license. But what's the catch? Let's run it...

#coding  #programming #programming

- TurboQuant

items.title: He just crawled through hell to fix the browser…
items.summary:

#coding #programming #typescript #javascript

- What makes dynamic text rendering so hard?

items.title: Jury rules Ticketmaster is an illegal monopoly #Vergecast
items.summary:

It wouldn't be Linus Tech Tips if we didnt try something stupid, and today we took the Falcon on a trip to Virginia and on the way decided to build a PC at 40,000 FT breaking a world record for the Highest PC build! But with the plane being as old as it is, how does power work? Can we even game on it once we are up there?

items.title: Nir Eyal | Beyond Belief | Talks at Google
items.summary:
Bestselling author and leading thinker on the psychology of behavior change and human potential, Nir Eyal, discusses his New York Times bestselling book Beyond Belief: The Science-Backed Way to Stop Limiting Yourself and Achieve Breakthrough Results, which reveals how the hidden assumptions you carry shape what you see, how you feel, and what you do—and how to replace them with beliefs that unlock your true potential.

Unlike the vague ‘just believe’ mantras, Nir offers a pragmatic, research-dri

items.title: Julia Minson | How to Disagree Better  | Talks at Google
items.summary:
Professor and behavioral scientist, Julia Minson, discusses her book How to Disagree Better, which reveals the counterintuitive secret to a life of less drama and more impact.

Julia brings to bear her decades of research into understanding the psychology of disagreement and its relevance to negotiations, conflict resolution, and decision-making, revealing the hidden skill that all the best mediators and negotiators share: displaying receptiveness to opposing views.

Get the book here: https://g

items.title: Vivian Tu | Well Endowed | Talks at Google

In this practical roadmap, Vivian blends Wall Street wisdom with her signature approachable style to guide you through adulthood

items.summary:

Whether used as a standalone guide or a companion to his acclaimed book, this workbook serves as a step-by-step roadmap for mastering high-stakes dialogue.

items.title: Jay Foreman & Mark Cooper-Jones | Map Men: This Way Up | Talks at Google

YouTube sensations and all-around cartographical nerds Jay Foreman and Mark Cooper-Jones—better known as Map Men—discuss their debut book, This Way Up: When Maps Go Wrong (and Why it Matters), a funny and fascinating journey into the maps that messed up: big time!

items.title: If you're job hunting, build software that removes friction from your own work
items.summary: If you're job hunting, build software that removes friction from your own work. That's a great demo to future employers that you can see a project through.

items.title: How to friction-max your learning with software engineer Jessica Rose [Podcast #216]
items.summary:
Today Quincy Larson interviews Jessica Rose. She's a dev and teacher who's worked on open data projects at Mozilla and lots of open source projects.

We talk about:
- How the whole world is hard, and how embracing that difficulty rather than avoiding it can make you a better thinker
- The Bad Website club, a free online bootcamp where people learn front end development together that starts this April
- Why building "silly little things" is one of the best things you can do as a learner

Links fr
